items.title: Haus Next.js patterns
items.purpose: Guide Next.js route, server/client boundary, and middleware-safe changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for App Router pages, layouts, route handlers, and middleware behavior updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for backend-only or non-Next framework tasks.

items.title: Haus React 19 patterns
items.purpose: Guide React component, hook, and state-boundary changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for React component behavior, hooks, and UI state flow updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for backend-only changes or framework routing config tasks.

items.title: Haus TypeScript 5 patterns
items.purpose: Guide TypeScript contract and migration-safe typing changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for shared types, DTOs, interface boundaries, and type-safe refactors.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for runtime-only changes without type-contract impact.

items.title: Haus Vite 8 patterns
items.purpose: Guide Vite config, plugin wiring, and deterministic build pipeline changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for vite config, alias/plugin, and build mode behavior updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for framework feature logic without build pipeline impact.

items.title: Haus React Router v7 patterns
items.purpose: Guide React Router v7 SSR route, loader, and action boundary changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for routes, loaders, actions, and SSR config in React Router v7 apps.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for Next.js App Router or Remix v1 codebases.

items.title: Haus Expo / React Native patterns
items.purpose: Guide Expo Router, React Native component, native module, and EAS build changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Expo Router routes, RN components, native modules, expo config plugins, and EAS build/submit.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for web-only React apps or unrelated mobile native projects (Swift/Kotlin without Expo).

items.title: Haus i18next patterns
items.purpose: Guide i18next setup, namespace structure, translation key conventions, and SSR-safe usage.
items.whenToUse: Use for adding/changing translations, configuring i18next, or wiring react-i18next / next-i18next.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-i18next translation systems or static-only content with no locale.

items.title: Haus BullMQ patterns
items.purpose: Guide BullMQ queue, worker, scheduler, and Redis-connection lifecycle changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for BullMQ queue definitions, processors, schedulers, and graceful shutdown.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-BullMQ job systems or in-process scheduling without Redis.

items.title: Haus Sentry patterns
items.purpose: Guide Sentry SDK setup, error capture, performance tracing, and PII scrubbing.
items.whenToUse: Use for `@sentry/*` SDK init, scope/breadcrumb config, source map upload, and PII redaction.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-Sentry monitoring (Datadog, New Relic, etc.).

items.title: Haus Prettier setup
items.purpose: Install `@haus-tech/tech-config` and wire `.prettierrc` to the shared prettier config via the `/prettier` subpath export.
items.whenToUse: Use when Prettier is missing from a haus repo, when migrating an ad-hoc config, or when migrating from the deprecated `@haus-tech/prettier-config`.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use when Prettier is already configured against `@haus-tech/tech-config/prettier`.

items.title: Haus ESLint setup
items.purpose: Install `@haus-tech/tech-config` and wire ESLint flat config to the shared rules.
items.whenToUse: Use when ESLint is missing from a haus repo or migrating from a legacy `.eslintrc` to flat config.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use when ESLint is already configured against `@haus-tech/tech-config`.

items.title: Haus Stripe patterns
items.purpose: Guide Stripe Elements, Checkout, webhook handling, and PCI-safe integration changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for `@stripe/stripe-js` / `@stripe/react-stripe-js` integrations, payment intents, webhooks, and Checkout.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-Stripe payment providers (Qliro, Klarna, etc.).

items.title: Haus Qliro patterns
items.purpose: Guide Qliro Checkout integration via @haus-tech/qliro-plugin in Vendure storefronts.
items.whenToUse: Use for Qliro Checkout flow, order callbacks, refund handling, and merchant API config.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-Nordic payment providers or Stripe-only flows.

items.title: Haus Supabase patterns
items.purpose: Guide Supabase client wiring, Row-Level Security, edge functions, and auth integration.
items.whenToUse: Use for `@supabase/supabase-js` queries, RLS policies, edge functions, and Supabase Auth flows.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-Supabase BaaS (Firebase, AWS Amplify) or self-hosted Postgres without Supabase.

items.title: Haus Sanity patterns
items.purpose: Guide Sanity v3/v5 schema, Studio config, GROQ query, and Next.js integration changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Sanity schema, Studio plugin, GROQ query, or next-sanity integration updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for Strapi, WordPress, or other CMS work.

items.title: Haus Strapi patterns
items.purpose: Guide Strapi v5 content type, controller, service, and policy changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Strapi content type schemas, controllers, services, and policies in v5.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for Sanity, WordPress, or other CMS work.

items.title: Haus Prisma patterns
items.purpose: Guide Prisma schema, migration, and query-safe ORM changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for prisma/schema.prisma updates, migrations, and PrismaClient query changes.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-Prisma ORM tasks or pure DB schema work without Prisma.

items.title: Haus Vendure plugin patterns
items.purpose: Guide Vendure plugin boundary, schema, and migration-safe development.
items.whenToUse: Use for Vendure plugin resolvers, services, entities, and plugin migrations.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for storefront-only UI changes or non-Vendure APIs.

items.title: Haus Laravel patterns
items.purpose: Guide Laravel domain, routing, policy, and persistence changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Laravel controllers/services/models/jobs/policies and route changes.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for Nova-only admin resource customization tasks.

items.title: Haus WordPress patterns
items.purpose: Guide WordPress hook/template/plugin/theme runtime changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for WordPress hook/filter/template/plugin/theme behavior updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for Bedrock-only config bootstrap or non-WP backend tasks.

items.title: Haus .NET patterns
items.purpose: Guide .NET API/service/domain/persistence layer changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for .NET service and endpoint behavior changes with domain impact.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for frontend-only or non-.NET runtime tasks.

items.title: Haus Playwright workflow
items.purpose: Guide deterministic E2E browser test flow changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Playwright specs, fixtures, and browser test stability work.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for unit-only test tasks without browser automation.

items.title: Haus Vendure app patterns
items.purpose: Guide Vendure app-level config, worker, bootstrap, and plugin wiring changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Vendure app config, worker, bootstrap, and system wiring updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for isolated shared plugin package tasks.

items.title: Haus NestJS GraphQL patterns
items.purpose: Guide resolver/module/guard/DTO-safe GraphQL implementation in NestJS.
items.whenToUse: Use for NestJS GraphQL resolver, module, DTO/input, and guard changes.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for REST-only controller tasks.

items.title: Haus Nx 21 monorepo patterns
items.purpose: Guide Nx project graph, target pipeline, and workspace boundaries.
items.whenToUse: Use for Nx workspace graph, target, and generator configuration updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for app-only feature edits without workspace impact.

items.title: Haus Turbo monorepo patterns
items.purpose: Guide Turbo pipeline and cache-safe monorepo orchestration changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for turbo pipeline graph and package task orchestration updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for package-local feature edits without pipeline impact.

items.title: Haus TanStack query/router patterns
items.purpose: Guide query key, invalidation, and route loader/data-flow changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for TanStack query cache behavior and TanStack router flow updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for styling-only component changes.

items.title: Haus Radix shadcn patterns
items.purpose: Guide accessible UI primitive and wrapper behavior changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Radix primitive usage and shadcn wrapper updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for backend/domain logic changes.

items.title: Haus Tailwind SCSS patterns
items.purpose: Guide style-system-safe Tailwind and SCSS module changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Tailwind token/utility and SCSS module behavior updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-style backend/service changes.

items.title: Haus Vue patterns
items.purpose: Guide Vue component/composable/state behavior changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for Vue component, composable, route, and state-boundary updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for React/Next-only component work.

items.title: Haus Laravel Nova patterns
items.purpose: Guide Nova admin resources, actions, and policy-safe field behavior.
items.whenToUse: Use for Laravel Nova resources, actions, filters, and admin UI behavior.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for customer-facing API/domain-only changes.

items.title: Haus WordPress Bedrock patterns
items.purpose: Guide Bedrock config/bootstrap and environment-aware WP deployment layout.
items.whenToUse: Use for Bedrock config, env loading, and boot path behavior updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for hook/template-only WordPress runtime changes.

items.title: Haus WordPress ACF Elementor JetEngine patterns
items.purpose: Guide builder-field/meta-template wiring for ACF, Elementor Pro, and JetEngine.
items.whenToUse: Use for ACF field groups and Elementor/JetEngine-driven dynamic template behavior.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for generic WordPress hooks with no builder/meta impact.

items.title: Haus dotnet service patterns
items.purpose: Guide .NET service-layer contract and orchestration changes.
items.whenToUse: Use for .NET service boundary, orchestration, and handler behavior updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for route formatting-only tasks with no service impact.

items.title: Haus enterprise auth patterns (OIDC / Azure AD / BankID / SAML2)
items.purpose: Guide identity-provider auth flow and claim/session lifecycle changes across OIDC, Azure AD, BankID, and SAML2.
items.whenToUse: Use for OIDC/Azure AD/BankID/SAML2 login, callback, token, claim mapping, and SP/IDP metadata updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for profile CRUD without auth protocol change.

items.title: Haus NextAuth patterns
items.purpose: Guide NextAuth.js / Auth.js provider, session, callback, and middleware changes in Next.js apps.
items.whenToUse: Use for `next-auth` / `@auth/core` provider setup, session strategy, callbacks, JWT, and route protection in Next.js.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for Laravel/PHP auth, OIDC outside Next.js, or session-less API tokens.

items.title: Haus database patterns
items.purpose: Guide schema/query/index/search changes across supported data engines.
items.whenToUse: Use for migrations, query behavior, indexing, and Elasticsearch mapping updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for UI-only changes without persistence/search impact.

items.title: Haus package manager patterns
items.purpose: Guide deterministic dependency/workspace changes for Yarn4 and pnpm89.
items.whenToUse: Use for lockfile, workspace, and package manager configuration changes.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for feature-only code edits with no dependency/workspace impact.

items.title: Haus testing library patterns
items.purpose: Guide user-visible behavior testing with Testing Library.
items.whenToUse: Use for component interaction tests with @testing-library tooling.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for full browser journey tests better served by Playwright.

items.title: Haus Vitest patterns
items.purpose: Guide Vitest unit/integration test structure, mocking, and coverage config.
items.whenToUse: Use when writing or modifying Vitest tests in TypeScript/JS frontends and Node services.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for Jest, Playwright, or PHPUnit test tasks.

items.title: Haus Jest patterns
items.purpose: Guide Jest unit/integration test structure, mocking, and Nx-style setup.
items.whenToUse: Use when writing or modifying Jest tests, including Nx workspace projects.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for Vitest, Playwright, or PHPUnit test tasks.

items.title: Haus PHPUnit patterns
items.purpose: Guide PHPUnit feature/unit test changes for PHP/Laravel behavior.
items.whenToUse: Use for PHPUnit tests tied to backend behavior updates.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for frontend/browser-only test tasks.

items.title: Haus Storybook patterns
items.purpose: Guide story coverage and component API documentation updates.
items.whenToUse: Use for component stories, args/controls, and Storybook config changes.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for backend/service-only development tasks.

items.title: Haus writing documentation
items.purpose: Create or refresh agent-operable project docs (CLAUDE.md, docs/SUMMARY.md, topic files) from code.
items.whenToUse: Use when the user runs the docs sync or asks to init/refresh project documentation for agent context, or after setup/commands/env/deploy/integration changes.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for code changes with no documentation impact, or where facts would have to be invented.

items.title: Agentic workflow standard
items.purpose: General agentic development workflow: feature loop, NEVER rules, testing standards, ADR, subagent patterns, security, accessibility.
items.whenToUse: Install in every project using haus-workflow.
items.whenNotToUse: Never skip this template.

items.title: Lefthook security pre-commit
items.purpose: Commit-time secret scanning (gitleaks + grep fallback) wired via Lefthook, complementing haus's runtime guards and settings.json deny rules.
items.whenToUse: Projects that want a commit-time secret-detection net alongside haus's real-time PreToolUse guards.
items.whenNotToUse: Repos that already run a secret-scanning pre-commit hook, or that cannot install gitleaks.

items.title: Superpowers Brainstorming
items.purpose: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
items.whenToUse: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for mechanical edits, bug fixes with known cause, or read-only questions.

items.title: Superpowers Checking Gates
items.purpose: Use when picking up a user-gate task OR when a hook demands re-validation. Runs the "do I know HOW?" self-check; if the HOW is clear, executes the verification and posts evidence; if not, hands off to specifying-gates. Kept deliberately separate from executing-plans so that without the opt-in hook, the main flow stays untouched.
items.whenToUse: Use when picking up a user-gate task OR when a hook demands re-validation. Runs the "do I know HOW?" self-check; if the HOW is clear, executes the verification and posts evidence; if not, hands off to specifying-gates. Kept deliberately separate from executing-plans so that without the opt-in hook, the main flow stays untouched.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use when the user-gate hook is not registered or the task is not user-gated.

items.title: Superpowers Dispatching Parallel Agents
items.purpose: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
items.whenToUse: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use when tasks share state or must run sequentially.

items.title: Superpowers Executing Plans
items.purpose: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
items.whenToUse: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use before a written plan exists or for single-step trivial changes.

items.title: Superpowers Finishing A Development Branch
items.purpose: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
items.whenToUse: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use while implementation or verification is still incomplete.

items.title: Superpowers Receiving Code Review
items.purpose: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
items.whenToUse: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use to blindly apply feedback without technical verification.

items.title: Superpowers Requesting Code Review
items.purpose: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
items.whenToUse: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for trivial one-line fixes with no review surface.

items.title: Superpowers Specifying Gates
items.purpose: Use when a user-gate task has requiresUserSpecification=true OR the agent's "do I know HOW?" self-check returns no. Locks down verification mechanics through a short AskUserQuestion sequence and writes the answers back into the task's metadata. Does NOT run the verification itself.
items.whenToUse: Use when a user-gate task has requiresUserSpecification=true OR the agent's "do I know HOW?" self-check returns no. Locks down verification mechanics through a short AskUserQuestion sequence and writes the answers back into the task's metadata. Does NOT run the verification itself.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use when verification mechanics are already specified or hook is off.

items.title: Superpowers Subagent Driven Development
items.purpose: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
items.whenToUse: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for tightly coupled sequential work or when subagents are unavailable.

items.title: Superpowers Systematic Debugging
items.purpose: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
items.whenToUse: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use to guess fixes without reproducing or investigating the failure.

items.title: Superpowers Test Driven Development
items.purpose: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
items.whenToUse: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for exploratory spikes, docs-only changes, or throwaway prototypes.

items.title: Superpowers Using Git Worktrees
items.purpose: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
items.whenToUse: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use when current workspace isolation is already sufficient.

items.title: Superpowers Using Superpowers
items.purpose: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
items.whenToUse: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
items.whenNotToUse: Do not skip when any skill might apply — this establishes the skill workflow.

items.title: Superpowers Verification Before Completion
items.purpose: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
items.whenToUse: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use to claim success before running the relevant verification commands.

items.title: Superpowers Writing Plans
items.purpose: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
items.whenToUse: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for single-step tasks that need no multi-step plan.

items.title: Superpowers Writing Skills
items.purpose: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
items.whenToUse: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for routine code changes unrelated to skill authoring.

items.title: Superpowers Brainstorm
items.purpose: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores requirements and design before implementation.
items.whenToUse: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores requirements and design before implementation.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for mechanical edits or read-only questions.

items.title: Superpowers Execute Plan
items.purpose: Execute plan in batches with review checkpoints
items.whenToUse: Execute plan in batches with review checkpoints
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use before a written plan exists.

items.title: Superpowers Gate Check
items.purpose: Run the 'do I know HOW?' self-check on a user-gate task and either execute its verification with captured evidence, or hand off to /specify-gate when the HOW is ambiguous. Dormant unless the user-gate opt-in hook is registered.
items.whenToUse: Run the 'do I know HOW?' self-check on a user-gate task and either execute its verification with captured evidence, or hand off to /specify-gate when the HOW is ambiguous. Dormant unless the user-gate opt-in hook is registered.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use when the user-gate hook is not registered.

items.title: Superpowers Specify Gate
items.purpose: Lock down verification mechanics for a user-gate task whose HOW is ambiguous. Asks 4 short questions, writes the answers back into the task's metadata, then returns control to executing-plans. Dormant unless the user-gate hook is registered.
items.whenToUse: Lock down verification mechanics for a user-gate task whose HOW is ambiguous. Asks 4 short questions, writes the answers back into the task's metadata, then returns control to executing-plans. Dormant unless the user-gate hook is registered.
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use when verification mechanics are already clear.

items.title: Superpowers Write Plan
items.purpose: Create detailed implementation plan with bite-sized tasks
items.whenToUse: Create detailed implementation plan with bite-sized tasks
items.whenNotToUse: Do not use for single-step tasks.

items.title: Superpowers Onboard
items.purpose: Guided setup for superpowers' optional features. Asks short multiple-choice questions and writes the chosen configuration files in place. Everything it configures can also be set up manually — see README.md.
items.whenToUse: Guided setup for superpowers' optional features. Asks short multiple-choice questions and writes the chosen configuration files in place. Everything it configures can also be set up manually — see README.md.
